data.body:
PREREQUISITE REALIGNMENT — reflect recent codebase changes (2026-06). Same process applied to the plan-review epic 5fd2: inventoried the shipped foundations (grounding oracle 8f6c, workflow engine e6d9, prompt storage da27) against this epic's assumptions, and corrected the description (new '## Prerequisite foundations — current codebase state' section + AC edits). Closed prerequisite epics NOT reopened.

NOW CONCRETE (was 'to build'):
- Native review_code ENGINE exists — src/rebar/llm/code_review.py + MCP review_code + 'rebar review-code'; deterministic applies_to selection → parallel reviewers → aggregate_findings → review_result. (Today SINGLE-PASS, not yet three-pass.)
- Provider-agnostic runner is the shipped PydanticAIRunner (src/rebar/llm/runner.py); LangGraph cut over (e6d9). 'Multi-provider runner' is now this concrete runner.
- The CODE reviewers (code_quality/security/tests) are front-matter contract-bearing prompts (da27): inputs/outputs contracts, execution_mode, dimension, applies_to globs (security.md → auth/secret/security/signing). Derived index + CI drift gate + Inspect-AI eval seam back them.
- Grounding oracle (8f6c) ships an EXTENSIBLE detector registry — the substrate for Pass-2 code-grounding.

STILL THIS EPIC'S WORK (clarified, not new scope):
- THREE-PASS: review_code is single-pass today; Pass-2 verifier + Pass-3 deterministic gate are the OPEN framework epic 9da1 (already a blocker/depends_on) this migrates review_code onto.
- SECRETS/SECURITY auto-block: the oracle registry is the substrate, but only smell_generic + applicability detectors ship today — NO secrets/security detectors yet; this epic adds them (or wraps gitleaks/semgrep behind the detector envelope).
- SOURCE-SEPARATION: review_code is inline in llm/code_review.py + env-gated (REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM) but not isolated.
- GERRIT LLM-Review voter: no Gerrit integration in-repo (prerequisite epic d251, also a blocker).

NOTE: the related framework epic 9da1 ('Realign reviews to three-pass + migrate existing reviewers') ALSO has prerequisite overlap — da27 already migrated the 6 reviewers to the front-matter contract model — so 9da1's 'migrate existing reviewers' premise has partly shifted; flagging in case you want 9da1 realigned too.
